ucs.title: [LLM] LazyScripter (G0140) IATA-themed mshta.exe execution pulling staged payloads from GitHub
ucs.description: Hunts LazyScripter's signature delivery chain: spearphishing emails (often impersonating IATA, BSPlink, or airline job-offer lures) drop or link to .hta/.vbs/.bat files that spawn mshta.exe or wscript.exe to fetch second-stage scripts from raw.githubusercontent.com or pastebin-style infra. This combination of airline-sector lure + LOLBin + GitHub staging is the actor's most consistent fingerprint per MalwareBytes (Feb 2021).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")) (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe")) AND (Processes.process IN ("*raw.githubusercontent.com*","*github.io*","*pastebin.com*","*paste.ee*","*.hta*","*IATA*","*BSPlink*","*Job*Offer*","*airline*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as conn_dest from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest IN ("*githubusercontent.com","*github.io","*pastebin.com") by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lures = dynamic(["IATA","BSPlink","airline","job offer","job_offer","CV","Resume","ticket","booking"]);
let stagers = dynamic(["raw.githubusercontent.com","github.io","pastebin.com","paste.ee","bitbucket.org"]);
let susParents = dynamic(["outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ ("mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (susParents)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (stagers) or ProcessCommandLine has_any (lures) or ProcessCommandLine has ".hta"
| extend stagerHit = iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any (stagers), 1, 0)
| extend lureHit   = iff(ProcessCommandLine has_any (lures), 1, 0)
| where stagerHit == 1 or (FileName =~ "mshta.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "http")
| join kind=leftouter (
    EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where Subject has_any (lures) or SenderFromDomain has_any ("iata","bsplink")
    | project NetworkMessageId, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, SenderFromAddress, EmailTimestamp=Timestamp
) on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, stagerHit, lureHit, Subject, SenderFromAddress
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: LazyScripter's reporting (MalwareBytes G0140) emphasises three pivot points other APTs rarely chain together at once: (1) airline / IATA / BSPlink job-offer lures (industry-specific), (2) mshta/wscript LOLBin execution from Office or browser parents, and (3) staging infrastructure on GitHub raw URLs and paste sites. Joining the email-lure context to the LOLBin process chain dramatically improves p

ucs.title: [LLM] LazyScripter open-source RAT loader chain (Koadic / Octopus / Quasar / LuminosityLink) via PowerShell from GitHub
ucs.description: Hunts LazyScripter's post-delivery installation pattern: a BAT/VBS dropper invoking PowerShell (often with -NoP -W Hidden -Enc) to download and run open-source RATs the group is uniquely fond of stacking together — Koadic, Octopus, Quasar, LuminosityLink, RMS, Remcos — frequently sourced from attacker-controlled GitHub repos and persisted via Run-key. The technique stack is generic on its own but the *combination* of GitHub staging + multi-RAT preference + Run-key persistence is LazyScripter-characteristic.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" AND (Processes.process IN ("*-nop*","*-NoP*","*-w hidden*","*-W Hidden*","*-enc*","*-EncodedCommand*","*FromBase64String*","*DownloadString*","*DownloadFile*","*Invoke-WebRequest*","*iex*","*IEX*")) AND (Processes.process IN ("*raw.githubusercontent.com*","*github.io*","*github.com*","*pastebin.com*")) AND (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.registry_value_data) as run_value values(Registry.registry_key_name) as run_key from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path IN ("*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*","*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce*") AND (Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*powershell*","*mshta*","*wscript*","*koadic*","*octopus*","*quasar*","*luminosity*","*remcos*","*rms*","*.hta*","*.vbs*")) by Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data] | stats values(*) as * by dest user | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let ratStrings = dynamic(["koadic","mshtml","stager.dll","Octopus","esharp","Quasar","client.exe","LuminosityLink","Remcos","RemoteUtilities","rutserv","njrat"]);
let stagers = dynamic(["raw.githubusercontent.com","github.io","github.com","pastebin.com","paste.ee"]);
let psSuspect = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "powershell.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-nop","-NoP","-w hidden","-W Hidden","-enc","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest","IEX","iex")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (stagers) or ProcessCommandLine has_any (ratStrings)
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let runKey = DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
| where RegistryValueData has_any ("powershell","mshta","wscript",".hta",".vbs",".bat")
   or RegistryValueData has_any (ratStrings)
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData;
psSuspect
| join kind=inner (runKey) on DeviceId
| extend timeDelta = abs(datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, Timestamp1))
| where timeDelta <= 60
| project DeviceName, AccountName, PsTime=Timestamp, ProcessCommandLine, RegTime=Timestamp1, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData
| order by PsTime desc
ucs.rationale: LazyScripter is unusual among tracked clusters in that it almost exclusively uses *open-source* RATs (Koadic, Octopus, Quasar, LuminosityLink, RMS, Remcos) rather than custom implants, and consistently retrieves them from GitHub repos under attacker control. Correlating an obfuscated PowerShell download from GitHub with a Run-key persistence event referencing one of those RAT names within the same
